text: The command center of the Eidolon. Consoles flicker with emergency lights.
A bank of failing displays shows corrupted star charts and static.
The captain's chair is overturned; a coffee mug lies shattered on the floor.
A maze of conduits and machinery. Humming engines provide power to the ship. Warning lights blink sporadically.
The engine room is filled with the scent of oil and ozone. A coolant leak hisses nearby.
Sparks fly from a damaged console. The floor vibrates with the ship's failing systems.
Crew quarters. Bunks line the walls, personal effects scattered about. The air smells of recycled oxygen.
The crew's living area. A communal table is overturned, dishes shattered. Personal lockers are open and empty.
The crew's quarters are eerily silent. A ventilation fan rattles intermittently, casting shadows on the walls.
Medical bay. Examination tables and diagnostic equipment. Emergency supplies are stored in cabinets.
The medbay is cold and sterile. A medical scanner flickers with corrupted data. A tray of instruments lies overturned.
The medical bay is quiet except for the soft beeping of inactive equipment. The air smells faintly of antiseptic.
Cargo hold. Crates and containers are secured to the floor. The space echoes with the hum of life support.
The cargo hold is dimly lit by industrial lamps. Loose straps sway gently, and a cold draft flows from an unknown source.
The cargo hold is filled with the scent of dust and machinery. A crate is dented from the inside, as if something tried to escape.
Airlock chamber. Heavy doors seal the entrance to space. Suits hang on racks, ready for EVA.
The airlock is cold and quiet. A suit visor is cracked, and warning lights blink in irregular patterns.
The airlock smells of cold metal and vacuum. A faint scraping sound comes from the outer door, as if something is trying to get in or out.
An empty corridor. Dim lights cast long shadows. The silence is broken only by distant machinery.
A desolate section of the ship. The air is cold and still. Shadows seem to move at the edge of vision.
An empty hallway stretches into darkness. A faint vibration runs through the floor, and something drips rhythmically in the distance.
